_meta: Chunk 1 — Qs 1-150. Covers Patterns, Conditionals, Parts of Speech, Voice, Verbals, Punctuation, Prepositions, Vocabulary, Idioms.

The passive voice of "Someone has stolen my wallet" is:
The passive voice of "The teacher is teaching the students" is:
questions.optionA: The students are being taught by the teacher.
questions.optionB: The students were taught by the teacher.
questions.optionC: The students are taught by the teacher.
questions.optionD: The students have been taught by the teacher.

questions.text: The passive voice of "They will build a new bridge" is:
questions.optionA: A new bridge will be built by them.
questions.optionB: A new bridge would be built by them.
questions.optionC: A new bridge was built by them.
questions.optionD: A new bridge is being built by them.

questions.text: The passive voice of "The police arrested the thief" is:
questions.optionA: The thief was arrested by the police.
questions.optionB: The thief is arrested by the police.
questions.optionC: The thief has been arrested by the police.
questions.optionD: The thief had been arrested by the police.

questions.text: The passive voice of "People speak English everywhere" is:
The passive voice of "She is writing a letter" is:
questions.optionA: A letter is written by her.
questions.optionB: A letter is being written by her.
questions.optionC: A letter was being written by her.
questions.optionD: A letter has been written by her.

questions.text: The passive voice of "He has finished the work" is:
questions.optionA: The work has been finished by him.
questions.optionB: The work was finished by him.
questions.optionC: The work had been finished by him.
questions.optionD: The work is finished by him.

questions.text: The passive voice of "They were painting the wall" is:
questions.optionA: The wall was being painted by them.
questions.optionB: The wall was painted by them.
questions.optionC: The wall has been painted by them.
questions.optionD: The wall is being painted by them.

questions.text: The passive voice of "You must respect your teachers" is:
questions.optionA: Your teachers must be respected by you.
questions.optionC: Your teachers must respect by you.

questions.text: The passive voice of "Who broke the window?" is:
questions.optionA: By whom was the window broken?
questions.optionC: By whom the window was broken?

questions.text: The passive voice of "Open the door" is:
The passive voice of "Don't touch the wires" is:
questions.optionA: Let not the wires be touched.

questions.text: The passive voice of "They are going to build a house" is:
questions.optionA: A house is going to be built by them.
questions.optionB: A house will be built by them.
questions.optionC: A house was going to be built.
questions.optionD: A house is built by them.

questions.text: The active voice of "The window was broken by the boy" is:
questions.optionB: The boy was breaking the window.
questions.optionC: The boy has broken the window.

questions.text: The active voice of "A letter was written by her" is:
The active voice of "The book is being read by him" is:
The active voice of "The thief has been caught by the police" is:
questions.optionB: The police has caught the thief.
questions.optionC: The police have caught the thief.
questions.optionD: The police are catching the thief.

questions.text: The passive voice of "He gave me a gift" is:
questions.optionA: A gift was given to me by him.
questions.optionB: I was given a gift by him.
questions.optionD: A gift was gave to me.

questions.text: The passive voice of "People say that he is honest" is:
questions.optionA: He is said to be honest.
questions.optionB: It is said that he is honest.

questions.text: The passive voice of "They know him to be a thief" is:
questions.optionA: He is known to be a thief.
questions.optionB: He is known as a thief.
questions.optionC: He was known to be a thief.
questions.optionD: He is being known to be a thief.

questions.text: The passive voice of "Who taught you French?" is:
questions.optionA: By whom were you taught French?

questions.text: The passive voice of "Please help me" is:
questions.optionA: You are requested to help me.

questions.text: The passive voice of "He keeps me waiting" is:
questions.optionA: I am kept waiting by him.
questions.optionB: I was kept waiting by him.
questions.optionC: I have been kept waiting by him.

questions.text: The passive voice of "They had completed the project" is:
questions.optionA: The project had been completed by them.
questions.optionB: The project has been completed by them.
questions.optionC: The project was completed by them.
questions.optionD: The project is completed by them.

questions.text: The passive voice of "He can solve this problem" is:
questions.optionA: This problem can be solved by him.
questions.optionB: This problem could be solved by him.
questions.optionC: This problem is solved by him.
questions.optionD: This problem must be solved by him.

questions.text: Which is a correct use of the semicolon?
questions.optionB: I like tea; however, she prefers coffee.

questions.optionA: My brother who lives in Delhi is a doctor.
questions.optionB: My brother, who lives in Delhi, is a doctor.
questions.optionC: My brother, who lives in Delhi is a doctor.
questions.optionD: My brother who, lives in Delhi, is a doctor.
Both B and C are possible.

questions.text: The colon is correctly used in:

questions.optionB: He bought the following items: apples, oranges, and bananas.
